The Funeral of the Emperor Constantine.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: The Funeral of the Emperor Constantine.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The body of the emperor was placed in a coffin of gold by the proper persons, and then conveyed to Constantinople, where it was laid out on an elevated bed of state in the palace, surrounded by a guard, and treated with the same respect as when he was alive, and this was done until the arrival of one of his sons.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: When Constantius was come out of the eastern parts of the empire, it was honored with an imperial sepulture, and deposited in the church called The Apostles: which he had caused to be constructed for this very purpose, that the emperors and prelates might receive a degree of veneration but little inferior to that which was paid to the relics of the apostles.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The Emperor Constantine lived sixty-five years, and reigned thirty-one.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He died in the consulate of Felician and Tatian, on the twenty-second of May, in the second year of the 278th Olympiad.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This book, therefore, embraces a period of thirty-one years.
